Fixed in frontend/src/ipc.ts: one TextDecoder per connection with {stream:true}. Verified end-to-end against a live PTY — 10x U+FFFD before, 0 after.

Not a font bug. The '?' glyphs were U+FFFD from UTF-8 sequences split across WebSocket frames (see the ipc.ts decode fix), not missing Nerd Font/powerline coverage. Glyph probe across all three renderers came back clean.
Fixed in frontend/src/tabs.ts: dedupe unchanged grid sizes + 80ms trailing debounce before notifying the PTY.

RESOLVED against ghostty-web. API surface: xterm.js exposes parser.registerOscHandler/registerCsiHandler/registerDcsHandler/registerEscHandler; ghostty-web exposes NO OSC handler registration at all — only OSC 8 hyperlinks, and its own types note the URI is 'not yet exposed in simplified API'. Verified empirically on xterm.js, not from docs: registered handlers for OSC 7 and OSC 133 and wrote real sequences — OSC 7 returned 'file://wemosd1/Users/shady/Documents/repos/nocx', OSC 133 returned all five markers A, B, C, D;0, D;127 including exit codes. So AD-5/AD-6 hold on xterm.js with no backend sniffing; on ghostty-web they would require patching the library.
Bake-off harness delivered its purpose — three renderers side by side, each with its own live PTY, drove the renderer decision. Tabs stay in the codebase for re-testing future candidates.

Fixed in internal/pty/pty_local.go: withUTF8Locale() defaults LANG=en_US.UTF-8 when the inherited environment states no locale at all (LC_ALL/LC_CTYPE/LANG all absent), which is the Finder/Dock launch case. An inherited locale is never overridden, including a deliberate LANG=C. Table-driven test covers all five cases; go test -race, gofumpt and golangci-lint all green.

Quality gates wired: .githooks/pre-commit (4 gates, verified red->green by the worker and re-verified independently by the coordinator), make hooks + make ci, .github/workflows/ci.yml (macos-latest backend, ubuntu frontend, golangci-lint pinned v1.64.8). First CI run still unverified — tracked separately.

ghostty-web dropped from the project (user decision 2026-07-21). These three bugs are ghostty-web-specific rendering defects (imageSmoothing/DPR blur, Retina cell seams from fractional canvas-2d rects, scroll reset on new output) and no longer describe any code we ship. xterm.js is the VT frontend per ADR-0001.

Superseded: the whole bead was 'verify ghostty-web exposes OSC 7/133, patch it if absent'. ghostty-web is dropped, and the spike nocx-dej already proved xterm.js exposes parser.registerOscHandler with OSC 7 and all five OSC 133 markers captured end-to-end (ADR-0001). Nothing left to verify or patch. The actual handler wiring lives in nocx-5mn.2 (OSC 7 cwd events) and nocx-5mn.3.

Done and verified by the coordinator: zero ghostty-web library references outside docs/decisions/, all grep survivors are competitor positioning; ADR-0001 untouched; TerminalRenderer + wterm intact; pre-commit hook green (gofumpt, golangci-lint, go test -race, tsc); npm run build succeeds. Not committed — conservative profile.
All three children complete: scaffold, composition root + slog, quality gates (hook + Makefile + GitHub Actions). First CI run still unverified — tracked in nocx-3xf.

Run 29822238195 (workflow_dispatch on main) green: backend 2m30s — gofumpt, golangci-lint v1.64.8, go build, go test -race all pass; frontend npm ci + tsc + vite build pass. Root cause of the first failure was golangci-lint-action@v4 shipping a Go-1.24-built binary against go.mod's go 1.26; fixed in 0adab78 by installing the linter with go install under setup-go's Go 1.26.

Control plane + session registry landed and reviewed over four rounds. JSON-RPC 2.0 open/close/resize + exit notification over text frames; binary data plane framed as version|msgType|16-byte session-id|payload with the metadata type reserved; server-authoritative ids from crypto/rand; PTY created at open's size (proven end-to-end via stty size); real session.Reg behind an injected PTYFactory (AD-8), concrete wiring at the composition root only. Spine updated: frontend is a peer quality surface, CI claims corrected to release-only. 64 tests passing, all gates green.

Frontend gated exactly like Go: eslint+prettier+vitest wired into pre-commit, make ci and CI; 39 frontend tests; connect() regression mutation-verified; golden wire vector pinned on both sides.

Frontend gated exactly like Go: eslint+prettier+vitest wired into pre-commit, make ci and CI; 39 frontend tests; connect() regression mutation-verified; golden wire vector pinned on both sides.

One WebSocket, N sessions: WSClient is multi-session with a per-session streaming TextDecoder; tabs share one client. Backend already multiplexed — proven by TestWSServer_TwoSessionsOneConnection_Isolation rather than rewritten.

Reconnect end to end: backend replay ring keyed by byte-offset with attach/ack, sessions survive a dropped WS; frontend tracks per-session offsets, throttled acks, reconnects with backoff and reattaches, handling resumed vs reset (full renderer reset + fresh decoder).

AD-10 credit flow control: per-session 64KB in-flight window derived from ring state, FairChunk yield so a flooding tab cannot monopolise the shared writer. Fixed a stall bug where the window waited for a fully-acked stream — a condition the client can never meet.

All five children complete: one WS with binary data plane + JSON-RPC control plane, server-authoritative session ids, multi-session multiplexing, AD-9 replay ring with reconnect, AD-10 per-session credit and fair scheduling.

Tabby-style tab bar: dynamic session-backed tabs, positional index badge matching Cmd+1..9, hover close, + button, activity indicator on background tabs, live OSC 0/2 titles via TerminalRenderer.onTitle. Renderer bake-off retired from the tab bar per ADR-0001; ?r= remains the switchable-renderer seam.

Real SSH client on x/crypto/ssh: known_hosts verification with unknown-vs-mismatch distinguished as typed errors, ~/.ssh/config resolution with option precedence, PTY created at the requested size including pixel dimensions sent as raw RFC 4254 payloads, tested against an in-process SSH server.

Not needed — wails dev already serves the whole app, bound Go methods included, at http://localhost:34115. The Vite-only port (5173) has no backend by definition; that was my mistake, not a missing seam. Browser-based UI checking and e2e go through 34115.

Closed

TitleBarHiddenInset's NSToolbar (UseToolbar) was what broke deminiaturize; TitleBarHidden keeps the hidden title and full-size content and restores correctly. Confirmed by the user on the real window.
Closed
Closed

Solved by the title-status layer: a braille spinner means working, Claude Code's ✳ means waiting, and a waiting background tab lights the underline. The OSC 133 dependency was my own modelling error — those markers are complementary (they cover shell command boundaries, which cannot see inside a long-running agent) and remain as nocx-5mn.4, not a blocker for this.

Verified by the repo owner in the real app: with one tab, clicking the window keeps the prompt and input works. Fixed by 25de485 (ResizeObserver repaints after clearing the texture atlas) and 2c02a46 (#panes sets --wails-draggable: no-drag, so Wails no longer swallows the mousedown). Guarded by e2e/click-focus.spec.ts for the shared click->focus->PTY path; see nocx-bq7 for what could not be guarded.

Fixed by gracefulShutdown: {signal: 'SIGTERM'} in playwright.config.ts. Root cause confirmed in the wails v2.13.0 source: the CLI starts the frontend watcher with Setpgid (dev_other.go:15) and reaps it from a SIGTERM handler via syscall.Kill(-pgid) (dev_other.go:32, dev.go:90/114). Playwright's default is to SIGKILL the group, so that handler never ran. Verified from a clean process table: 4 passed in 15.7s, 16.4s wall clock (was 3m26s for fewer tests), and no wails dev, app or vite process left behind.

Beads now syncs from .githooks/: pre-commit exports and stages .beads/issues.jsonl, pre-push runs bd dolt push. One hook root, both files tracked, wired by the existing 'make hooks'.

Two design assumptions were wrong and were corrected by measurement, not argument:
1. 'bd hooks run pre-commit' does NOT force a JSONL export here — it exits 0 and changes nothing. pre-commit calls 'bd export -o' directly instead, which is exact and synchronous. export.auto: true is still enabled, but only to keep the file warm between commits: it is throttled to 60s and the write does not reliably land before the command returns.
2. 'bd hooks run pre-push' is a silent no-op on bd 1.0.5 — exit 0 with refs/dolt/data unmoved. 'bd dolt push' moves it. The hook calls that. Worth re-testing on the next bd upgrade, since the shim is the documented interface (Homebrew already offers 1.1.0 against the 1.0.5 installed here).

Failure policy is hybrid and was tested against all three branches with a stub bd: missing bd -> skip (0), exit 3 no database -> skip (0), genuine failure -> block with the fix spelled out. This differs from the stock beads shim, which ends in 'exit $_bd_exit' and blocks git on any error.

Verified end to end: a fresh clone plus 'bd bootstrap' now reports nocx-d1f CLOSED, where before this change the same clone reported it as an open P0 while the issues.jsonl beside it said closed.

Verified by the repo owner in the real WKWebView app, which is the only place this reproduces: resize no longer mangles glyphs, close-tab-then-type shows no red garbage, and nocx-d1f has not regressed (one tab, click, type renders correctly) — that last check mattered because the fix removes the clearTextureAtlas() call that 25de485 had paired with a refresh.

Fix: refreshAtlas() in frontend/src/renderers/xterm.ts now only calls term.refresh(0, rows-1); the clearTextureAtlas() call is gone, because clearing before repainting races with atlas repopulation in _updateModel while the resize path already refreshes the char atlas via _refreshCharAtlas().

No automated guard ships, deliberately. Both Playwright engines were tried with the fix reverted — Chromium and WebKit, WebGL confirmed active in each — and NEITHER reproduces the corruption. A test that passes in both states is a false guard, so none was written; the finding is recorded in the notes instead. Our e2e harness is structurally blind to this class of bug.
Closed
Closed

The constructor no longer paints FALLBACK_TITLE into the DOM; the label stays empty until openSession resolves with the directory name. Guarded by e2e/tab-title.spec.ts, which snapshots the label via a MutationObserver at the instant the tab node is inserted — before the fix that snapshot read 'Terminal'. Passes in both Playwright engines.

Verified by the repo owner in the real WKWebView app: the title stays at full strength and fades only under the close button. Fixed by dropping -webkit-mask entirely in favour of an absolutely-positioned ::after gradient overlay (34px, right edge, fading to the tab's current background via a --tab-bg custom property, z-index 1 under the close button's 2). Masks had failed twice here — first two layers compositing unpredictably, then a single gradient dimming the whole label — because WKWebView's defaults for mask-size/mask-repeat diverge from Chromium's. An ordinary positioned gradient has no engine-dependent behaviour.

Landed in 2c02a46 (commit body already said 'Closes nocx-cpp' but the bead was never closed). Frameless via mac.TitleBarHidden; .tabbar is the title bar with 78px left padding and --wails-draggable: drag, children set back to no-drag. Deviation from the acceptance wording: traffic lights are NOT inset — TitleBarHiddenInset's NSToolbar broke deminiaturize (nocx-dqg, P0), so TitleBarHidden was chosen and the padding clears the lights at their default position.

Superseded by the frameless work in 2c02a46, which is the second branch of this bead's own acceptance criteria. The element that lightened was the native macOS title bar; mac.TitleBarHidden removes it entirely, so there is nothing left to highlight. No dedicated fix commit exists — checked the whole log, nothing references this bead. Confirmed in style.css: hover rules exist only on .tab, .tab-close and .tab-add; .tabbar itself has none.
